name: OpenRouter
description: Routing gateway with one API over hundreds of models, unified billing, and fallback

provider.comment: Operated by OpenRouter, Inc. (founded early 2023 by Alex Atallah).

category.comment: Unified API fanning out to hundreds of models across many providers with automatic fallback/routing — the defining gateway shape.

api-launched.comment: Approximate — OpenRouter was founded and first exposed its unified API in early 2023 (Feb/Mar); no exact GA date is published. Checked 2026-07-24.

status.comment: Developer API is generally available and self-serve.

auth-methods.comment: Primary auth is a bearer API key; OAuth (PKCE) login is available, notably for app integrations and the MCP server.

self-serve-signup.comment: Sign up and generate an API key without sales contact; top up credits self-serve.

free-credits-trial.comment: Small free allowance for new users, plus free-tier models (50 requests/day without credits, 1,000/day with $10+ balance). Checked 2026-07-24.

regional-endpoints.comment: Single global endpoint (openrouter.ai/api/v1); a managed EU-jurisdiction endpoint (eu.openrouter.ai) is available to enterprise customers by request.

data-residency-options.comment: Enterprise 'Sovereign AI' EU region locking / in-region routing pins processing jurisdiction; enterprise-tier / on request.

model-breadth.comment: Reaches models from many vendors (Anthropic, OpenAI, Google, Meta, Mistral, DeepSeek, Qwen, etc.) through one API.

open-weight-models.comment: Open-weight families (Llama, Mistral, Qwen, DeepSeek, etc.) are available alongside proprietary models.

model-version-pinning.comment: Models are addressed by explicit vendor/model slugs (e.g. meta-llama/llama-4-scout); specific dated snapshots can be pinned where the upstream vendor offers them.

custom-fine-tuned-deployment.comment: OpenRouter routes to catalog models; it does not host/deploy customer-uploaded fine-tuned weights. BYOK lets you route to your own provider keys, but that is not native custom-model deployment.

model-catalog-size.comment: Roughly 400+ models across 70+ providers as marketed on the enterprise page. Checked 2026-07-24.

tool-function-calling.comment: Standardized tool/function-calling interface across supported models.

parallel-tool-calls.comment: parallel_tool_calls parameter (default true for most models) enables multiple tool calls per turn.

structured-output.comment: Server-enforced JSON Schema via response_format type json_schema with optional strict mode (strongest); valid-JSON mode also supported. Enforcement depends on the selected provider/endpoint.

image-input-vision.comment: Vision-capable models accept images via URL or base64.

audio-input.comment: Speech-capable models accept audio input; a transcription (speech-to-text) endpoint is also offered.

document-pdf-input.comment: Native PDF/document ingestion with parsing of text and scanned files.

image-generation.comment: Image-generation output supported through the same API.

text-to-speech-audio-output.comment: Dedicated text-to-speech endpoint generating MP3/PCM audio.

embeddings-endpoint.comment: POST /api/v1/embeddings, OpenAI-compatible; multiple embedding models available.

prompt-caching.comment: Automatic caching on providers like OpenAI/DeepSeek/Groq; explicit cache_control breakpoints for Anthropic/Google. Provider-sticky routing maximizes cache hits.

batch-api.comment: No dedicated asynchronous offline batch/bulk job endpoint (like OpenAI's 24h Batch API); requests are per-call. Embeddings accept multiple inputs per request but that is not an offline batch job.

max-context-window.comment: OpenRouter lists Llama 4 Scout at 1M tokens (native architecture claims up to 10M). Several catalog models offer ~1M+ context. Checked 2026-07-24; varies per model/provider.

extended-reasoning.comment: Unified reasoning parameter normalizes effort levels and reasoning-token budgets across providers (OpenAI effort, Anthropic token budgets, Gemini thinking levels).

built-in-web-search.comment: First-party web plugin (append :online to any model slug) returning url_citation annotations; native search for Anthropic/Google/OpenAI/Perplexity/xAI, Exa for others.

built-in-code-execution.comment: No first-party provider-hosted code-interpreter/sandbox exposed via the API; you wire your own execution as a tool.

mcp-support.comment: No native hosted/remote MCP connector; docs show a client-side bridge converting MCP tool definitions to OpenAI-compatible tools rather than server-side MCP support.

files-retrieval-api.comment: No managed files/retrieval (RAG) API; documents are passed inline per request. Embeddings are offered but retrieval orchestration is left to the caller.

agents-assistants-api.comment: Offers a client-side Agent SDK for tool orchestration, but no server-side stateful assistants/threads API with managed conversation state.

openai-compatible-api.comment: Drop-in OpenAI SDK replacement via base-URL swap to https://openrouter.ai/api/v1.

official-sdks.comment: First-party TypeScript (@openrouter/sdk) and Python SDKs; both documented as beta as of the check date. OpenAI/other libraries also work via the compatible endpoint.

native-api-style.comment: Native request/response shape mirrors OpenAI Chat Completions (a Responses-style API is in beta).

webhooks-callbacks.comment: No documented webhook/async callback mechanism for job completion.

published-sla.comment: Enterprise page states a commitment to being 'accretive to your uptime' but publishes no contractual/quantified uptime SLA percentage.

rate-limit-model.comment: Limits scale with credit balance (e.g. free-model daily caps rise from 50/day to 1,000/day at $10+ balance); no fixed per-tier plan caps.

provider-fallback-routing.comment: Automatic cross-provider load balancing and fallback by default (allow_fallbacks), avoiding recently-unstable providers — the defining gateway capability.

status-page.comment: Public status page at status.openrouter.ai (current status only; no historical incident archive).

no-training-default.comment: OpenRouter itself does not train on prompts, but by default requests may route to underlying providers that train on data; an account-level opt-out ('disable training') routes only to non-training providers. Because the default may include training providers, marked false per guideline.

zero-data-retention.comment: Zero Data Retention routing enforces requests to only ZDR-guaranteed providers/endpoints (account-wide, per-model-group, per-API-key); requests fail rather than violate policy. Coverage varies by provider.

compliance-certifications.comment: Enterprise page states SOC-2 compliance and GDPR (signed DPA available on enterprise tier). Request current attestations from the trust portal. Checked 2026-07-24.

enterprise-sso-access-control.comment: Organization support with SSO and org-level guardrails/allowlists.

pricing-model.comment: Per-token passthrough of underlying provider pricing (no markup on inference) plus free-tier models; revenue via credit-purchase fees (Stripe 5.5%/$0.80 min, crypto 5%) and a 5% BYOK fee — the 'markup' tag reflects those gateway fees.

unified-billing.comment: One USD credit balance covers every reachable model/provider.

cache-batch-discount.comment: Cached-prompt reads are discounted (roughly 0.1x–0.5x of input cost depending on provider; cache writes typically ~1.25x).

cheapest-entry.comment: Free-tier models and a small free new-user allowance let you start with no payment; pay-as-you-go credits thereafter.
